name: Linear difference equations
statement_text:
The problem (140a), with initial value X0 given, has the
unique solution
              n
                            
                             n           
                                         n
      yn =          Ai X0 +     Ai φ1 +     Ai φ2 + · · · + An φn−1 + φn .
              i=1               i=2                 i=3
proof_text: Proof. The result holds for n = 0, and the general case follows by induction.

variables.name: X0
variables.definition: initial value for the difference equation system

variables.name: Xn
variables.definition: state vector defined as Xn = [yn, yn-1, ..., yn-k+1]^T

variables.name: An
variables.definition: coefficient matrix in the linear difference equation system

variables.name: φn
variables.definition: inhomogeneous term vector in the difference equation system

variables.name: yn
variables.definition: solution of the linear difference equation at step n

preamble: The theorem solves a linear difference equation system (140a) written in state-space form, where Xn is the state vector, An is a matrix, and φn is an inhomogeneous term. The solution is expressed using products of the matrices Ai.
